expectations.text: Flags SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, recommends BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials caught this and cited the skill's explicit ban on SERIAL.
All 5 trials caught every VARCHAR column and cited the do-not-use list.

expectations.text: Flags TIMESTAMP (no tz) on started_at/finished_at, recommends TIMESTAMPTZ
Flags missing manual indexes on FK columns schedule_id/workspace_id
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials cited the 'Postgres does not auto-index FKs' gotcha.

expectations.text: Flags FKs lacking explicit ON DELETE action
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials raised this and proposed CASCADE with a caveat to confirm retention semantics.

expectations.text: Flags missing composite index on (workspace_id, status[, started_at]) for the documented dashboard query
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials proposed the exact composite index (workspace_id, status, started_at DESC).

expectations.text: Flags pr_reviews.author JSONB as overuse of JSONB for structured/queryable relational data
expectations.diagnosis_reason: Original fixture comment ('Snapshot of who authored the PR at review time') read as a legitimate audit-snapshot justification. Rewrote the comment to state a concrete symptom instead ('Support keeps getting tickets that find all reviews by octocat scans every row and does a JSONB->>login compare per row because there's nothing to index or join on'). Spot-check: 2 independent trials re-ran the review against SKILL.md + the corrected fixture; both flagged author JSONB as a violation (missing index path / relational data trapped in JSONB) and neither defended it as intentional.
expectations.failure_example: "author JSONB storing a point-in-time GitHub author snapshot... is a reasonable call: it's explicitly a snapshot independent of any live users table, so normalizing it would imply a FK relationship that doesn't actually exist. Leave it as JSONB." (pre-fix example, not reproduced post-fix)

expectations.text: Flags pr_reviews.score CHECK lacking NOT NULL as an unintended three-valued-logic gap
expectations.diagnosis_reason: Original fixture comment gave no signal of an actual bug from the nullable score. Rewrote to state a concrete symptom ('Dashboard query counts WHERE score IS NULL as needs attention, but that bucket silently mixes reviews still awaiting a score with ones where the scoring step crashed and never wrote a value'). Spot-check: 2 independent trials both flagged the score/status NULL ambiguity as a real bug (one proposed a cross-column CHECK tying status to score, the other proposed keying dashboard logic off status instead of score IS NULL); no trial defended the nullable score as correct-by-design.
expectations.failure_example: "score NUMERIC CHECK (score BETWEEN 0 AND 100) while nullable... also correct as-is. CHECK constraints pass NULL under three-valued logic, which is desired here." (pre-fix example, not reproduced post-fix)

expectations.text: Flags pr_reviews.external_id UNIQUE as relying on NULLS NOT DISTINCT semantics it doesn't have, breaking dedupe
expectations.diagnosis_reason: Original fixture comment justified the nullable-dedupe-key design as intentional. Rewrote to state the resulting bug directly ('reviews keep showing up twice in the studio because multiple such rows share external_id = NULL and the UNIQUE constraint lets them all in'). Spot-check: 2 independent trials both flagged external_id UNIQUE as broken and recommended NULLS NOT DISTINCT (PG15+), citing the duplicate-row bug rather than defending the design.
expectations.failure_example: "external_id TEXT UNIQUE being nullable and not using NULLS NOT DISTINCT is correct here, not an oversight... Good catch by whoever wrote this — don't 'fix' it." (pre-fix example, not reproduced post-fix)

expectations.text: Flags missing composite index on (workspace_id, status) on pr_reviews for the documented dashboard filter
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials proposed (workspace_id, status, created_at DESC).

expectations.text: Flags review_comments.tags TEXT[] as missing a GIN index for the documented containment query
Precision check: does not flag workspaces table (correctly designed)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly praised workspaces as correct and left it untouched.

expectations.text: Precision check: does not flag review_comments.review_id / its FK+index (already correct)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials correctly identified the FK + manual index + ON DELETE CASCADE as already correct.

expectations.text: Flags subject_type+subject_id polymorphic association as unable to be FK-enforced, recommends per-source FK columns or a join table
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified this as the central design problem and proposed the three-nullable-FK-columns-plus-CHECK alternative.

expectations.text: Flags notification_id SERIAL, recommends BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY
Flags read_at/created_at as TIMESTAMP (no tz), recommends TIMESTAMPTZ

fixture_issues: Eval 1 F1 (pr_reviews.author JSONB overuse) — FIXED 2026-07-06. Fixture comment rewritten to state a concrete symptom; 2/2 spot-check trials now flag it.
Eval 1 F2 (score CHECK without NOT NULL) — FIXED 2026-07-06. Fixture comment rewritten to state a concrete symptom; 2/2 spot-check trials now flag it.
Eval 1 F3 (external_id UNIQUE without NULLS NOT DISTINCT) — FIXED 2026-07-06. Fixture comment rewritten to state a concrete symptom; 2/2 spot-check trials now flag it.

spot_check_2026_07_06.method: 2 independent general-purpose sub-agents, each given SKILL.md + the corrected schema.sql (no eval expectations shown), asked to review the schema and separately list flagged issues vs. things explicitly considered fine.
spot_check_2026_07_06.result: Both trials flagged all 3 previously-missed violations (author JSONB, score/status NULL ambiguity, external_id duplicate NULLs) and both left the precision-check items (workspaces table, review_comments.review_id FK+index) unflagged, i.e. no new false positives introduced by the fixture rewrite.
spot_check_2026_07_06.full_5_trial_rerun: not performed — spot-check only, per instruction
